shape: 1:N branch-attach (three independent attaches, one shared anchor card)
description: Wave-84 deferred wires applied. BT4-030 At All Costs Vegeta attaches to the existing [[vegeta]] node (SSB-form Vegeta, canonical fourth aspect under the parent — adds a Colossal Warfare BT4 anchor to the multi-set roster). BT4-097 Instant Transmission attaches to [[son-goku]] as a signature-technique card per the Goku's Lineage [Permanent] rules-text and the canonical two-finger-forehead visual identifier. BT3-028 Grand Tour Spaceship triple-attaches to [[pan]] + [[son-goku]] + [[trunks]] as the canonical GT traveling-trio quartet anchor (Giru already wired to machine-mutants-m2 in prior wave); the card's rules-text explicitly searches for 'Son Goku: GT', 'Trunks: GT', 'Pan', or 'Giru', and the three-figure foreground composition matches the trio. All three attaches required only frontmatter additions on the card side plus appears_on + Appears-on-listing updates on each character node; no new edges in card bodies, no new structural primitives.
notes: Three independent branch-attaches in one pass; clean from-the-slate work, no edges in card bodies. Pan / Goku / Trunks nodes each gain one anchor; Vegeta and Goku each gain one. BT3-028 is the rare triple-attach — clean because Bandai's rules-text explicitly names the trio as the card's mechanical search targets, making the canonical-naming load-bearing rather than tag-thin. Edgelord prefers a clean 1:1 mirror, but these are the deferred wires from wave 84 and the work was load-bearing.
